learning_outcomes.description: Analyse the structure, operation and performance of contemporary electricity generators and electricity systems.

Compare and assess renewable, low carbon and conventional electricity generation technologies including the technical principles, performance characteristics, advantages, and limitations of key energy conversion and storage technologies relevant to sustainable energy systems.

Synthesize integrated sustainable electricity systems that balance engineering technical feasibility with economic viability, environmental sustainability and social acceptance within the Australian context.
Effectively communicate to multiple audiences and stakeholders, sustainable engineering solutions to multifaceted energy problems

assessments.weighting: <dd>20% each problem set is worth 4%. We take the best 5 of 8 possible submission </dd>

assessments.task_description:
Students will complete a problem set on a particular course topic. There are a maximum of 8 possible problem sets.

For each student we will identify the 5 submissions with the highest mark which will then be aggregated (for a total mark out of 20%).

Problem sets published on Blackboard. Students will submit individually.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The complete project description will be posted to Blackboard. 

This project will focus on electricity generation using renewable electricity generators and energy storage systems. The project will be completed individually.

Students will be required to design a system that balances electricity demand with supply, determine economic performance and environmental / social impacts using appropriate metrics and attempt to incorporate realistic elements of grid operation for an Australian location. 

The format of the task will be a report, with all associated calculations / technical information included as appendices.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Reports should be submitted through Blackboard. Students are welcome to submit their calculation and analysis files if desired.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The final exam will assess all topics covered in the course including through lectures, tutorials and other assessment items.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Guest Lectures

Engineers, academics and other professionals working in the Energy and Engineering sectors will be invited to give seminars as guest lecturers. The aim of this activity is to show the students the current problems faced in Australia and internationally and the likely pathway that the industry is taking to solve these problems. Attendance is expected as the guest lecturers volunteer to present.

Typical topics may include:

  • Project development lifecycles
  • Energy project case studies
  • Energy markets

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

Attendance is expected as the guest lecturers volunteer to present. Typical topics may include: Project development lifecycles Energy project case studies Energy markets

learning_activities.topic:
Core Lectures

Core lectures delivered by the teaching team focus on the electricity system and its transition to a sustainable system. This includes lectures on renewable electricity generators, thermal electricity generators, carbon dioxide reduction technologies as they pertain to electricity generation, energy storage technologies, integrated systems, economic and environmental metrics / indicators.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorials

Tutorial time focus primarily on assessable problem sets related to the lecture content. Some time may be spent on project work and/or final exam preparation.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
